content.text: This is the STOP condition the task anticipated. The pinned home-manager (rev `abfad3d`, release 26.11, 2025-04-24) provides the `hyprcursor.enable` option, but in this version it **only sets `HYPRCURSOR_THEME`/`HYPRCURSOR_SIZE` env vars** — it does **not** run hyprcursor-util or generate any hyprcursor theme (no `manifest.hl`/`hyprcursors/`). So the built-in option alone won't produce a real hyprcursor theme for the xcursor-only Bibata. Let me investigate hyprcursor-util's actual conversion capability before implementing the equivalent.

content.text:
Decisive finding at line 170/205:
```cpp
if (NAME != name && name != themeDir.path().stem().string())
    continue;
```
hyprcursor matches a theme if **either** the manifest `name` **or** the directory stem equals the requested name. And a dir is only considered if it contains `manifest.hl`/`manifest.toml` (line 61) — so the xcursor-only `icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice` dir is invisible to hyprcursor and won't collide.

So the design is: keep the built-in `hyprcursor.enable` (it sets `HYPRCURSOR_THEME=Bibata-Modern-Ice`), and generate a real hyprcursor theme whose `manifest.hl` `name = Bibata-Modern-Ice`, installed in a data dir hyprcursor searches. Let me write it into `desktop.nix`.

lines: -    # Hyprland 0.55 defaults to hyprcursor, but bibata-cursors ships only an
-    # XCursor theme. Without a hyprcursor theme Hyprland can't resolve Bibata
-    # and falls back to a generic default cursor, so have home-manager convert
-    # the XCursor theme into hyprcursor format (size inherits from `size`).
+    # Hyprland 0.55 uses hyprcursor by default, but bibata-cursors ships only an
+    # XCursor theme, so Hyprland can't resolve Bibata and falls back to a generic
+    # default cursor. Enabling hyprcursor here exports HYPRCURSOR_THEME/SIZE so
+    # Hyprland looks for a hyprcursor theme named "Bibata-Modern-Ice" (this
+    # home-manager version only sets the env vars; the theme itself is generated
+    # from the XCursor theme in the xdg.dataFile entry below).
+  # bibata-cursors is XCursor-only, so convert it to hyprcursor format for
+  # decompiles the XCursor theme (needs xcur2png) and --create compiles a

input.command:
cd /tmp/klaus-sessions/cosmo/20260711-1313-b7ae7d79
cat > /tmp/pr-body.md <<'EOF'
## Problem

After a reboot activated a pending system upgrade, the Hyprland cursor on
`classic-laddie` (and any host using this home profile) changed to a generic,
default-looking cursor instead of the configured Bibata.

## Root cause (verified on the live system)

- The cursor is configured in `home/desktop.nix` via `home.pointerCursor` as
  `Bibata-Modern-Ice` (`pkgs.bibata-cursors`), but only as an **XCursor** theme
  (`gtk.enable = true`; no hyprcursor). The `bibata-cursors` package ships
  XCursor format only — `cursors/` + `index.theme`, no `manifest.hl`.
- Hyprland 0.55.4 (current on the host) defaults to **hyprcursor**
  (`cursor:enable_hyprcursor = 1`, confirmed via `hyprctl`). With no hyprcursor
  theme available, Hyprland cannot resolve Bibata and falls back to its built-in
  default cursor — exactly the symptom the upgrade surfaced.

## Fix

`home/desktop.nix`, `home.pointerCursor` block only, plus one generated theme:

1. `home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable = true;` — exports
   `HYPRCURSOR_THEME=Bibata-Modern-Ice` / `HYPRCURSOR_SIZE=24`.
2. A generated hyprcursor theme installed under
   `~/.local/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor`, built by converting
   Bibata's XCursor theme with `hyprcursor-util` (`--extract` via `xcur2png`,
   then `--create`), with the manifest `name` set to `Bibata-Modern-Ice`.

### Why the extra step (important)

The pinned home-manager in this flake (`abfad3d`, release 26.11, locked
2025-04-24) implements `home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable` as **env vars
only** — it sets `HYPRCURSOR_THEME`/`HYPRCURSOR_SIZE` but does **not** run
`hyprcursor-util` or generate any hyprcursor theme (see
`modules/config/home-cursor.nix`). So the option alone does not fix the
xcursor-only Bibata. This PR therefore also generates the actual hyprcursor
theme, as the task's fallback path anticipated.

hyprcursor resolves a theme when either the `manifest.hl` `name` **or** the
directory stem matches the requested name (`libhyprcursor/hyprcursor.cpp`:
`if (NAME != name && name != themeDir.path().stem().string()) continue;`), and
only considers dirs that contain a `manifest.hl`. Setting the manifest name to
`Bibata-Modern-Ice` makes Hyprland pick this theme, while the original
XCursor `icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice` dir (no manifest) is ignored by hyprcursor
and left untouched for GTK/X apps.

## Verification

- `nix eval …home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable` → `true`.
- `home.sessionVariables` → `HYPRCURSOR_THEME=Bibata-Modern-Ice`,
  `HYPRCURSOR_SIZE=24`.
- Built the generated theme derivation directly and inspected the output:
  `manifest.hl` with `name = Bibata-Modern-Ice` and `hyprcursors/` containing
  **56 compiled `.hlc` shapes**.
- `xdg.dataFile."icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor".target` →
  `.local/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor` (a dir libhyprcursor
  searches).
- `nix build --dry-run` of
  `.#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel`
  evaluates cleanly (no eval errors introduced).
- `nixfmt` / `treefmt`: 0 changes; pre-commit hooks and `klaus _pre-review`
  pass with no findings.

### Note on the full toplevel build

`nix build …toplevel` currently fails on an **unrelated, pre-existing** issue:
`catppuccin-gtk` → `python3.14-catppuccin-2.5.0` breaks because
`matplotlib.style` has no `core` attribute. This reproduces identically on the
unmodified tree (same derivation hash), so it is not caused by this change and
is orthogonal to the cursor fix.

## After merge

Rebuild, then re-login (or run `hyprctl setcursor Bibata-Modern-Ice 24`) for
the cursor to take effect.

PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/611

## Summary

Fixed the Hyprland default-cursor fallback on `classic-laddie` by generating a hyprcursor-format Bibata theme in `home/desktop.nix`.

**Root cause (confirmed):** Hyprland 0.55 defaults to hyprcursor, but `bibata-cursors` ships XCursor format only — so Hyprland couldn't resolve `Bibata-Modern-Ice` and fell back to its generic built-in cursor.

**Key discovery during implementation:** The task's suggested one-liner (`hyprcursor.enable = true`) is *necessary but not sufficient* here. I traced the pinned home-manager (`abfad3d`, release 26.11) and found its `hyprcursor.enable` only exports `HYPRCURSOR_THEME`/`HYPRCURSOR_SIZE` env vars — it does **not** run `hyprcursor-util` or generate any theme. This is exactly the STOP/fallback condition the task described. So I:

1. Kept `hyprcursor.enable = true` (sets the env vars pointing Hyprland at the theme name).
2. Added an `xdg.dataFile` that generates a real hyprcursor theme by converting Bibata's XCursor theme with `hyprcursor-util` (extract via `xcur2png` → create), with the manifest `name` set to `Bibata-Modern-Ice`.

I verified against the actual hyprcursor library source (`hyprcursor.cpp`) that themes match on either manifest name *or* directory stem, and that manifest-less dirs are ignored — so the generated theme resolves cleanly while the original XCursor dir stays untouched for GTK/X apps.

**Verified:**
- Built the theme derivation directly → `manifest.hl` with `name = Bibata-Modern-Ice` + 56 compiled `.hlc` shapes.
- Env vars and dataFile target correctly wired; full config evaluates cleanly (`--dry-run` passes).
- `nixfmt`/`treefmt` clean; pre-commit hooks and `klaus _pre-review` pass with no findings.

**One honest caveat surfaced in the PR:** the full `toplevel` build currently fails on an **unrelated, pre-existing** `catppuccin-gtk`/`python3.14-catppuccin` matplotlib break — reproduced identically on the unmodified tree (same derivation hash), so it's orthogonal to this change. I documented this rather than claiming a full build success.

The PR notes the user should re-login (or run `hyprctl setcursor Bibata-Modern-Ice 24`) after rebuild.
